REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JESSICA ESMAY, JESSICA ESMAY AS TRUSTEE OF THE DELORISA TURNER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE COURT NOTES THAT BOTH SIDES CHOSE NOT TO COMPLY WITH PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ADJUDICATION. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B) (COMPARE NOTICE OF MOTION AND SEPARATE STATEMENT) AND FAILED TO TAB ITS PAPERS CONTAINING MULTIPLE EXHIBITS; AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROPERLY RESPOND TO THE SEPARATE STATEMENT; FAILED TO PROVIDE OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE IN THE PROPER FORMAT; DELIVERED A DOBLE-SIDED COURTESY COPY. THE COURT WILL ADDRESS THE MOTION ON THE MERITS. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT THEIR BURDEN AND THERE ARE MULTIPLE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACTS. THE FACTS INCLUDED IN DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT ARE NOT SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE AND/OR ARE DISPUTED: 10, 12, 16, 17, 19, 20 AND 29. FURTHERMORE, THERE IS A DISPUTE REGARDING THE PARTIES' RIGHTS, OBLIGATIONS AND PERFORMANCE UNDER PARAGRAPH 12 OF THE LEASE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTED, ASHLEY CAVDEK, CSR 14610, REPORTERASHLEY@GMAIL.COM, 562-243-0966 (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

David Semel Esq., Fried & Williams LLP, 415-421-0100 via zoom for plaintiff
Andrew McClellan Esq., Lunski & Stimpert LLP, 415-426-3525 in-person for defendant Jessica Esmay

Mr. McClelland constests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JESSICA ESMAY, JESSICA ESMAY AS TRUSTEE OF THE DELORISA TURNER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. The Court notes that both sides chose not to comply with procedural requirements for motions for summary judgment adjudication. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) (Compare Notice of Motion and Separate Statement) and failed to tab its papers containing multiple exhibits; and Plaintiff failed to properly respond to the separate statement; failed to provide objections to evidence in the proper format; delivered a doble-sided courtesy copy. The Court will address the motion on the merits. The motion is denied as the moving party failed to shift their burden and there are multiple triable issues of facts. The facts included in Defendant's separate statement are not supported by evidence and/or are disputed: 10, 12, 16, 17, 19, 20 and 29. Furthermore, there is a dispute regarding the parties' rights, obligations and performance under paragraph 12 of the lease.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported, Ashley Cavdek, CSR 14610, reporterashley@gmail.com, 562-243-0966 (D501)
